classification.full: Classification: Snack-Sensitive · For Authorized Personnel Only

home.title: WRAFF FORCE
home.subtitle: K9 Division · Snack Security · Est. 2025
home.tagline: Paws on the Ground. Noses in the Air. Snacks Secured.
home.missionSummary: Wraff Force is a specialized K9 tactical unit operating wherever the global snack supply is at risk. Established in 2025 following an incident in Berlin that remains only partially classified.

about.title: AGENCY
about.lead: Wraff Force is a specialized K9 tactical unit operating wherever the global snack supply is at risk. Established in March 2025 following an incident in Berlin that remains only partially classified. We do not discuss the details. The pretzels were unrecovered. We moved on.
about.foundingBody1: On the afternoon of March 14, 2025, a pretzel stand in central Berlin was left unguarded for a duration of 4 minutes and 17 seconds. When supervision resumed, three pretzels, one bag of peanuts and one chocolate bar were missing. A single unidentified pawprint was found in the salt shaker. No witnesses came forward. The case was closed as unexplained snack loss.
about.foundingBody2: Puppy Treatz, primary operative and founding member, was present at the scene. The incident was filed. The implications were noted. Three weeks later, Wraff Force was established. The pretzel stand no longer exists. This is officially attributed to renovation. No further questions are accepted on this matter.
about.mandateText: To secure, protect and where necessary recover the global snack supply. No biscuit left behind. No kibble left to fate. Wraff Force answers every call.
about.tl1body: An unguarded pretzel stand in Berlin was left unsupervised for 4 minutes and 17 seconds. Three pretzels, one bag of peanuts and one chocolate bar vanished. One unidentified pawprint was recovered from the salt shaker. No witnesses. Case closed as unexplained snack loss. Wraff Force founded three weeks later.
about.tl2body: Unit Two, Sergeant Bork and Lieutenant Sniff join as founding operatives. Corporal Zoom joins two weeks later after a door-related incident delayed their onboarding by 14 incident reports.
about.tl3body: During a routine sweep, Unit Four located a small brown teddy bear alone in an open field. The bear was named Colonel Flops without prior authorization. Command approved without explanation. Colonel Flops was assigned to eastern gate. He has not moved since. Unit Four also walked into a wall on the same mission. Both events are in the report.
about.tl4body: File sealed. WF-TREAT-06. We do not discuss June 2025. Operatives seeking further information are redirected to the snack inventory checklist. That is the only response available.
about.tl5body: Agent Muffin joins on probationary status following the highest enthusiasm rating ever recorded during onboarding. One clean mission completed. Filed under: Anomaly. Unit Five joins Cuddle Command, immediately locates Colonel Flops and holds him for 3 minutes and 40 seconds. This is noted in both their files.
about.tl6body: First large-scale public deployment at Folsom Europe, Berlin. Full snack perimeter established and held. Colonel Flops deployed to eastern gate — the rabbit hesitated. A non-regulation squeaky toy was encountered in Sector Two. Unit Two's after-action report remains sealed. Crowd reception: enthusiastic.
about.tl7body: Annual Easter security deployment. Chocolate reserves secured at 73% recovery rate. Unit Two found with foil wrapper fragments. Explanation filed. Review ongoing. The Easter Bunny has been formally classified as a hostile presence.
about.tl8body: Nature of incident: under investigation. A kitchen is involved. No further statement will be issued at this time.
about.doctrineIntro: Wraff Force operates under a strict set of tactical and behavioral principles. These are not suggestions. They are doctrine. Several sections of the Field Manual are still being finalized. This does not affect operational readiness.
about.doctrine1body: All snacks under Wraff Force jurisdiction are to be treated as mission-critical assets. No operative may consume a secured snack without explicit handler authorization. Consumption without authorization is logged. It is always logged.
about.doctrine2body: A snack recovery rate of 73% is the established benchmark for mission success. This figure was determined organically during Operation Crumb Recovery and has never been revised. It will not be revised.
about.doctrine3body: Colonel Flops holds the rank of Colonel. This rank is recognized. His counsel is sought. His silence is interpreted. He has never confirmed or denied any interpretation. This has not stopped anyone from asking.
about.doctrine4body: All incidents — regardless of scale, cause or level of embarrassment — are to be documented and filed within 24 hours. 23 reports filed to date. 17 involve Unit Two. All explanations were technically plausible.
about.doctrine5body: This is not a guideline. This is doctrine. The holographic treat incident is classified under WF-TREAT-06. The file is sealed. There is nothing further.
about.doctrine6body: Unit Five has established that cuddles with Colonel Flops constitute a legitimate operational activity. Command reviewed this assertion. Command filed it. The duration is recorded. Everyone has moved on.
about.value1body: Unwavering commitment to the unit, the handler and the mission. In that order. Unless treats are present. Then the order may vary. This is acknowledged in the Field Manual.
about.value2body: Every movement is deliberate. Every sniff is intentional. Every report is filed accurately. Except Appendix B. Appendix B is redacted. This will not change.
about.value3body: Wraff Force operates with full transparency — within classification guidelines. What can be disclosed, is disclosed. What cannot, is redacted. The redactions are always necessary.
about.value4body: Wraff Force has endured squeaky toys, holographic treats, foil wrapper incidents and the ongoing Habinghorst Kitchen Situation. We are still here. We are still filing reports. We will not stop.
about.value5body: The protection of snack reserves is not merely a mission objective. It is a calling. No operative joined Wraff Force by accident. The snacks called to them. They answered.
about.value6body: If it happened, it is filed. If it is filed, it exists. If it exists, it is subject to review. Unit Two is aware of this. This has not changed Unit Two's behavior in any measurable way.
about.statementText: Someone had to do it. The snacks were not going to protect themselves. We looked at the gap. We looked at each other. We looked at the snacks. We formed a unit. We filed the paperwork. Wraff Force exists because it had to.
about.fieldManualDesc: Official operational regulations. 12 articles. Several sections incomplete. Squeaky toy protocol: not started.
about.operativesDesc: Full unit roster. All 8 active operatives. Dossiers, status and behavioral profiles. Unit Two file: under ongoing review.
about.incidentDesc: 23 filed incidents. Some sealed. All technically explained. WF-TREAT-06 is not available at standard clearance level.

operatives.title: OPERATIVES
operatives.lead: Complete roster of all active Wraff Force operatives. Access individual dossiers for full behavioral profiles, incident history and gear loadout. All files are current. One file is under ongoing review.
operatives.classifiedNote: Additional operative files exist at higher clearance levels. Access restricted. Contact your handler for authorization.
operatives.cardBody: Access complete dossiers for all 8 active Wraff Force operatives. Status, division, behavioral profile and incident history.
operatives.treatzNote: Primary operative and founding member. Present at the Berlin Pretzel Incident. All subsequent Wraff Force activity traces back to this moment.
operatives.flopsNote: Found in an open field. Named without authorization. Assigned to eastern gate. Has not moved. Two commendations. Both accepted in silence.
operatives.unitTwoNote: 17 incident reports filed. All explanations technically plausible. Consistently first to arrive at snack-adjacent locations. Review ongoing.
operatives.borkNote: Cannot hear the words walk, walkies or outside without immediately leaving the area. Command has built all walks into the mission timeline.
operatives.sniffNote: Cannot leave a scene until the scent is fully analyzed. Reports average 4 pages in length. Delayed one mission by 22 minutes over a cheese biscuit.
operatives.zoomNote: Fastest operative in the unit. 14 door-related incident reports filed. Enthusiasm has not decreased following any of them. Annex 7-A under revision.
operatives.muffinNote: Highest enthusiasm rating ever recorded during onboarding. One clean mission completed. Filed under: Anomaly. Status: Probationary. Enthusiasm: unaffected.
operatives.unitFiveNote: Named Colonel Flops. Cuddles with Colonel Flops are described as contractual. Duration is recorded. Command has reviewed this. Command has filed it. Everyone moved on.

dossier.treatzSummary: Founding member and primary operative of Wraff Force. Present at the Berlin Pretzel Incident, March 2025. Established the unit in response. All Wraff Force activity traces back to this individual. Active in the pupplay community in North Rhine-Westphalia and beyond. Open to contact with fellow operatives and pups worldwide.
dossier.treatzStrength1body: Demonstrates exceptional situational awareness in snack-adjacent environments. First to identify unguarded treat scenarios. Response time: immediate.
dossier.treatzStrength2body: Founded and maintains Wraff Force as a cohesive tactical unit. Operational structure, mission doctrine and 73% recovery benchmark all originate here.
dossier.treatzWeakness1body: Documented difficulty maintaining operational composure in the presence of unsecured pretzels. Origin of the Berlin Incident. Filed. Not forgotten.
dossier.treatzWeakness2body: Initial operative contact classified as: reserved. Approach with patience. Full engagement protocol activates once trust threshold is reached. At that point, disengagement is not recommended.
dossier.treatzGagBody: Founded a global snack security unit in direct response to a snack incident they were present for. Command has noted the irony. Command has not acted on it.
dossier.treatzGag2Body: Consistently draws new contacts to the unit through event presence and tactical outfit visibility. Treats as currency. Gear as introduction. Unit Two was not recruited this way. Unit Two just appeared.
dossier.treatzM1body: Present at the founding incident. Pretzel stand unsupervised for 4 min 17 sec. Three pretzels, one chocolate bar unaccounted for. One pawprint recovered. Wraff Force established three weeks later as direct response.
dossier.treatzM2body: First large-scale public deployment. Led snack perimeter operation at Folsom Europe. Perimeter held. Colonel Flops deployed to eastern gate on personal authority. The rabbit hesitated. Mission outcome: successful.
dossier.treatzM3body: Annual chocolate reserve protection deployment. 73% recovery rate maintained. Easter Bunny formally classified as hostile following operational contact. Unit Two found with foil wrapper fragments. Review ongoing.
dossier.treatzM4body: April 1st snack integrity operation. Holographic treat protocol reviewed and applied. Treat authenticity confirmed before approach. All operatives maintained composure. The June 2025 incident was not repeated.
dossier.treatzI1: Present at unguarded pretzel stand, Berlin. Duration: 4 min 17 sec. Three pretzels, one chocolate bar missing. One pawprint recovered. Explanation: I was in the area. Resulted in founding of Wraff Force.
dossier.treatzI2: Field manual Section 7 (Squeaky Toy Protocol) noted as not started for the third consecutive review period. Commitment to completion: reiterated. Progress: unchanged. Status: ongoing.
dossier.treatzI3: Colonel Flops commissioning authorized without formal review process. Name approved without documented rationale. Eastern gate assignment made verbally. All subsequent paperwork filed retroactively.
dossier.treatzNetworkIntro: Primary operative maintains documented contact with all active Wraff Force units. Network connections are operational in nature. One connection is under ongoing review. Colonel Flops has not confirmed or denied any of these relationships.
dossier.treatzNet1: Commissioned by Treatz without prior authorization. Relationship: foundational. Status: officially acknowledged.
dossier.treatzNet2: Under observation. 17 incident reports. Treatz maintains professional distance near secured snacks.
dossier.treatzNet3: Reliable perimeter anchor. The word walkies is not used in briefings. This was learned the hard way.
dossier.treatzNet4: Most thorough field analyst in the unit. Mission timelines now include a 25-minute sniff buffer.
dossier.treatzNet5: Fastest response time in the unit. All doors on deployment sites are now pre-assessed.
dossier.treatzNet6: Named Colonel Flops. Cuddle protocol filed and approved. Treatz signed off on it. No comment issued.
dossier.priorServiceText: Prior service record is classified at a level exceeding current access clearance. Requests for further information have been submitted. Responses have not been received. Colonel Flops has been asked directly. Colonel Flops did not answer. This was interpreted as confirmation. This interpretation is not official.
dossier.flopsSummary: Found alone in an open field during a routine sweep, May 2025. Named Colonel Flops by Unit Four without prior authorization. Assigned to eastern gate by command without documented rationale. Has not moved. Has not spoken. Has not needed to. Two commendations issued. Both accepted in silence. Prior service record: classified.
dossier.flopsStrength1body: Documented deterrent effect on hostile contacts at eastern gate. The rabbit hesitated at Folsom. No further explanation has been offered. None has been requested.
dossier.flopsStrength2body: Has never abandoned his post. Has never filed a complaint. Has never required debriefing. Zero incident reports originating from eastern gate. Benchmark for all other operatives.
dossier.flopsStrength3body: Has never confirmed or denied any intelligence assessment, relationship or operational interpretation. Button eyes convey nothing. This has been described as deeply unsettling by Unit Two. Filed.
dossier.flopsWeakness1body: Colonel Flops does not move independently. Deployment requires physical relocation by another operative. This has never been described as a problem. It has simply been noted.
dossier.flopsWeakness2body: Has submitted zero written or verbal field reports to date. Command has interpreted his silence as satisfactory. No further reporting requirements have been issued.
dossier.flopsGagBody: Colonel Flops silence is the single most reviewed document in the Unit Two investigation. Analysts have examined it 34 times. No conclusions have been drawn. The silence remains pending.
dossier.flopsCom1note: The rabbit hesitated. Commendation issued. Accepted in silence.
dossier.flopsCom2note: 365 days at post. No deviations. Commendation issued. Accepted in silence.
dossier.flopsM1body: First large-scale public deployment. Assigned to eastern gate on personal authority of Puppy Treatz. Held position for full duration. One rabbit approached. The rabbit hesitated. The rabbit withdrew. Colonel Flops did not move. Colonel Flops did not need to.
dossier.flopsM2body: Deployed as psychological anchor during April 1st integrity operation. No operative attempted a false treat claim while Colonel Flops was watching. This is considered significant. It has not been officially documented as such.
dossier.flopsM3body: Deployed at primary chocolate reserve perimeter. Unit Two was found at the perimeter. Unit Two noted Colonel Flops was present. Unit Two filed an explanation. The explanation did not mention Colonel Flops. This omission has been noted.
dossier.flopsI1: Commissioned without formal review. Named without documented authorization. Assigned to eastern gate verbally. All paperwork filed retroactively. Status: closed.
dossier.flopsI2: Unit Five located Colonel Flops and held him for 3 minutes 40 seconds. Described as contractual. Colonel Flops permitted this. Duration recorded. No objection filed. No comment issued.
dossier.flopsI3: Colonel Flops was relocated from eastern gate for 4 minutes during perimeter cleaning. Upon return, three operatives reported feeling watched. Colonel Flops had not moved. He was not yet back. This incident is under review.
dossier.flopsNetworkIntro: Colonel Flops has not confirmed any of the following relationships. Colonel Flops has not denied any of the following relationships. All assessments below are based on field observation and are considered unofficial.
dossier.flopsNet1: Responsible for naming, commissioning and eastern gate assignment. Colonel Flops has not thanked him. Colonel Flops has not complained either.
dossier.flopsNet2: Colonel Flops silence is the most reviewed document in the Unit Two investigation. Unit Two has described the silence as unhelpful. The silence is ongoing.
dossier.flopsNet3: Named Colonel Flops. Cuddles are described as contractual at 3 min 40 sec per session. Colonel Flops has permitted every session. This is the closest thing to a confirmed relationship on file.
dossier.flopsNet4: Has saluted Colonel Flops on three separate occasions. Colonel Flops did not return the salute. Bork saluted again anyway.
dossier.flopsNet5: Attempted full scent analysis on two occasions. Results: polyester, cotton filling, trace amounts of field dust. Report: 4 pages. Conclusions: inconclusive.
dossier.flopsNet6: Attempted to give Colonel Flops a treat on first day. The treat was placed in front of him. The treat remained there for 6 hours. It was eventually filed as an unsecured snack incident.
dossier.unitTwoSummary: 17 incident reports filed. All explanations technically plausible. Consistently first to arrive at snack-adjacent locations. Beef breath index elevated on 17 of 19 occasions. Investigation ongoing. No conclusion has been reached. Unit Two is aware of this.
dossier.unitTwoS1b: Consistently arrives at snack locations faster than any other operative. Response time to unsecured treat scenarios: under 90 seconds. Mechanism: unknown.
dossier.unitTwoS2b: All 17 incident reports contain technically plausible explanations. Not a single explanation has been disproven. Lt. Sniff has called this statistically implausible. The reports remain open.
dossier.unitTwoW1b: Elevated readings on 17 of 19 occasions. Correlation with snack-adjacent incidents: 89%. Unit Two explanation: dietary variance. Lt. Sniff assessment: this is a pattern.
dossier.unitTwoW2b: Cannot be within 2 metres of secured snack reserves without triggering a proximity alert. Protocol introduced after incident 7. Has not reduced incident frequency.
dossier.unitTwoG1b: Unit Two is always the first operative to arrive at any snack-related incident. Always. Command has noted this. Unit Two has an explanation. Command is reviewing it.
dossier.unitTwoG2b: Multiple operatives have reported that Unit Two gives a specific look when near secured snacks. The look has been described as innocent. Lt. Sniff: that is not what innocent looks like.
dossier.unitTwoM1b: Deployed for snack recovery. Achieved 73% recovery rate. Remaining 27% unaccounted for. Unit Two present for full duration. Report filed. Contents reviewed.
dossier.unitTwoM2b: After-action report sealed. Foil wrapper fragments found on Unit Two post-mission. Explanation: pre-existing environmental contamination.
dossier.unitTwoM3b: Deployed at chocolate reserve perimeter. Colonel Flops also present. Unit Two report did not mention Colonel Flops. This omission was noted.
dossier.unitTwoI1: Found within 1 metre of secured biscuit reserve. Explanation: checking perimeter integrity. Accepted. 16 similar incidents followed.
dossier.unitTwoI2: Beef breath index: critical. Timing: 4 minutes after snack inventory discrepancy. Explanation: dietary variance. Lt. Sniff: this is incident 11.
dossier.unitTwoI3: Colonel Flops' silence reviewed as evidence. Unit Two present during review. Unit Two did not comment. This was noted.
dossier.unitTwoNetworkIntro: Unit Two maintains operational contact with all active Wraff Force units. Several connections have been cross-referenced with the ongoing investigation. No conclusions have been drawn. Unit Two has not been informed of this cross-referencing.
dossier.unitTwoNet_treatz: Primary operative. Filed the proximity watch protocol. Maintains professional distance from Unit Two near secured snacks.
dossier.unitTwoNet_colonel_flops: Most reviewed document in the investigation is Colonel Flops silence. Relationship status: unconfirmed. Colonel Flops has not commented.
dossier.unitTwoNet_sergeant_bork: Perimeter anchor. Reliable. Has not filed any incident reports involving Unit Two. This is noted.
dossier.unitTwoNet_lieutenant_sniff: Primary analyst on the Unit Two investigation. Has filed 12 scent reports. Each concludes: this is a pattern.
dossier.unitTwoNet_corporal_zoom: No documented snack incidents shared. Zoom is too fast to be implicated. Unit Two finds this professionally inconvenient.
dossier.unitTwoNet_unit_five: No overlap in incident history. Unit Five is always near Colonel Flops. Colonel Flops is always at eastern gate. Eastern gate has no snacks.
dossier.borkSummary: Founding operative. Most reliable perimeter anchor in the unit. Cannot process the words walk, walkies or outside without immediately vacating the area. Command has incorporated all walks into the official mission timeline.
dossier.borkS1b: Holds position with absolute consistency. Has never abandoned a post for any reason other than the W-word. That exception is documented and accepted.
dossier.borkS2b: German Shepherd in full tactical vest. The perimeter holds itself in Bork's presence. No further analysis required.
dossier.borkW1b: Triggers immediate departure upon hearing walk, walkies or outside. Range: up to 40 metres. Command does not use these words in briefings.
dossier.borkW2b: Three briefings ended early due to accidental W-word usage. New protocol introduced. Incident rate: reduced. Not eliminated.
dossier.borkG1b: All walks are now logged as tactical perimeter surveys. Bork departs on schedule. Returns on schedule. The mission timeline has never been cleaner.
dossier.borkG2b: Bork's ears rotate independently toward any sound. Useful for threat detection. Has caused three false alarms involving a crisp packet.
dossier.borkM1b: Held outer perimeter for full duration. Three W-word incidents from adjacent civilians. All three gaps covered by Corporal Zoom.
dossier.borkM2b: Eastern perimeter anchor. No W-word incidents recorded. One civilian said the word walks nearby. Bork's ear rotated. He did not move. Commendation issued.
dossier.borkI1: Abandoned inner perimeter post. Civilian said: going for a walk. Absence: 12 minutes. Post covered by Corporal Zoom.
dossier.borkI2: Responded to W-word during active snack perimeter. Post temporarily unmanned. No snack loss. Classified as near-miss. Protocol updated.
dossier.borkNetworkIntro: Sergeant Bork maintains solid operational relationships across the unit. The word walkies has been removed from all network communication logs at Bork's request. This was not formally requested. It was understood.
dossier.sniffSummary: Most analytically thorough operative in the unit. Cannot leave a scene until the scent profile is fully documented. Reports average 4 pages. One mission delayed 22 minutes over a non-mission-relevant cheese biscuit. The report was filed anyway.
dossier.sniffS1b: Identifies snack type, age, trajectory and probable operative from a single trace. Accuracy rate: 94%. The remaining 6% involves Colonel Flops. Results: inconclusive.
dossier.sniffS2b: Field reports are the most detailed in the unit. Average: 4.2 pages. Unit Two's file is 31 pages.
dossier.sniffW1b: Cannot leave until full analysis complete. Average delay: 14 minutes. Maximum: 22 minutes over a cheese biscuit that was not mission-relevant.
dossier.sniffW2b: Documented sensitivity to cheese biscuit proximity. One mission delayed. One report filed. The biscuit was described as highly complex.
dossier.sniffG1b: Primary analyst on the Unit Two investigation. 12 supplementary scent reports filed. Each concludes: this is a pattern. No formal action has followed.
dossier.sniffG2b: Two scent analyses attempted on Colonel Flops. Results: polyester, cotton filling, field dust. Filed as inconclusive. Third attempt pending clearance.
dossier.sniffM1b: Full scent sweep completed. 4 unsecured cheese items identified. Mission delayed 22 minutes on an unidentified biscuit. Not mission-relevant. Report filed anyway.
dossier.sniffM2b: Post-mission scent sweep. Foil wrapper traces identified on Unit Two. 6-page report filed. Conclusion: this is a pattern.
dossier.sniffI1: Mission delayed 22 minutes. Cheese biscuit of unknown origin. Full analysis conducted. Non-mission-relevant. Report filed regardless. 4 pages.
dossier.sniffI2: Unauthorized scent analysis of Colonel Flops during active deployment. Paused at eastern gate 8 minutes. Results: inconclusive. Third attempt pending.
dossier.sniffNetworkIntro: Lt. Sniff has filed scent assessments on every operative in the network. Most assessments are inconclusive. Unit Two's assessment is the exception. Unit Two's assessment is 31 pages long.
dossier.zoomSummary: Fastest operative in the unit. 14 door-related incident reports on file. Enthusiasm has not decreased following any of them. All doors on active deployment sites are now pre-assessed by command before Corporal Zoom is given access. Annex 7-A is under ongoing revision. Formerly designated Unit Four, 04-K9-OPS. The designation was retired informally following the third door incident. No formal renaming ceremony was held. The name Zoom was in general use before the paperwork was filed. The paperwork was eventually filed.
dossier.zoomS1b: Fastest response time in the unit. Standby to position: 3.2 seconds. Has covered Sergeant Bork's post during every W-word incident without exception.
dossier.zoomS2b: 14 door incidents. Zero reduction in enthusiasm. Command has described this as both remarkable and logistically challenging. Zoom calls it readiness.
dossier.zoomW1b: Collides with doors at an above-average rate. Ajar, closed or unexpected — all equally at risk. Annex 7-A addresses this. Annex 7-A is not yet complete.
dossier.zoomW2b: Occasionally arrives at a location before the briefing finishes. Has been present at two destinations before the team departed. Coordination: ongoing.
dossier.zoomG1b: Each door incident has a separate report. Each notes that enthusiasm was unaffected. The doors have not become safer. Zoom has not commented.
dossier.zoomG2b: Annex 7-A addresses rapid response door protocols. Under revision since June 2025. The squeaky toy section and Annex 7-A are the two incomplete items. They may be related.
dossier.zoomM1b: Covered Sergeant Bork's post three times during W-word incidents. Response time each: under 4 seconds. One door incident en route. Mission unaffected.
dossier.zoomM2b: Responded to two alerts within deployment window. Both cleared before arrival. Third alert was a squeaky toy. Zoom arrived first. The toy was addressed.
dossier.zoomI1: Door incident 1. Sliding door — not fully open. Response velocity exceeded door clearance speed. Annex 7-A begun.
dossier.zoomI2: Door incident 14. Glass door — clean. Not visible at approach velocity. Enthusiasm: unaffected. Annex 7-A revision: ongoing.
dossier.zoomNetworkIntro: Corporal Zoom maintains strong operational bonds across the unit. Has covered every operative's position at some point. Usually arrives before being asked.
dossier.muffinSummary: Highest enthusiasm rating ever recorded during onboarding. One clean mission completed. Filed under: Anomaly. The anomaly has not been explained. Muffin has not been asked to explain it. Probationary status maintained. Enthusiasm: unaffected.
dossier.muffinS1b: Highest onboarding score in Wraff Force history: 98/100. The 2-point deduction was for sitting on the mission briefing document. Muffin was very sorry.
dossier.muffinS2b: One clean mission on record. No incidents. No snack losses. Full perimeter. Filed under Anomaly. Command is still reviewing how this happened.
dossier.muffinW1b: 19 missions attempted. 1 fully clean. 18 with noted anomalies. The anomalies share one common element. That element has not been formally identified.
dossier.muffinW2b: Attempted to offer Colonel Flops a treat on day one. Treat remained uncollected for 6 hours. Filed as unsecured snack incident WF-IR-019.
dossier.muffinG1b: Every incident begins with good intentions clearly documented. Command has noted this. It has not reduced the incident count. It has made the reports more enjoyable to read.
dossier.muffinG2b: Mission WF-OP-016 was flawless. No explanation found. Muffin was asked about it. Muffin wagged. The wagging was not an official response but has been noted.
dossier.muffinM1b: First active deployment. Three biscuits consumed during duty. Each filed as an accident. All three times.
dossier.muffinM2b: Full perimeter maintained. No incidents. No snack losses. Filed under Anomaly. Muffin was asked what happened. Muffin wagged.
dossier.muffinI1: Three biscuits consumed during active perimeter duty. Three separate reports. Three explanations: it was an accident. Probationary status review initiated.
dossier.muffinI2: Attempted to share treat with Colonel Flops. Treat uncollected for 6 hours. Filed as unsecured snack incident. Muffin expressed remorse. This was noted.
dossier.muffinNetworkIntro: Agent Muffin approaches all network relationships with maximum enthusiasm. This is appreciated. It is also sometimes logged.
dossier.unitFiveSummary: Found Colonel Flops on the walk back from the first deployment. Named him on the spot. The naming was not authorized. Command approved it. Unit Five visits Colonel Flops at the eastern gate on a regular basis. Cuddle duration is logged at 3 minutes 40 seconds per session. This has been described as contractual.
dossier.unitFiveS1b: Documented positive effect on morale following all cuddle sessions. Operatives near eastern gate report improved outlook. This has not been officially measured. It has been widely observed.
dossier.unitFiveS2b: Samoyed fur volume has proven useful for obscuring tactical vest details during civilian operations. Three missions cite fur density as a contributing factor.
dossier.unitFiveW1b: Locates Colonel Flops within 4 minutes of arriving at any site. Every deployment. Eastern gate is now Unit Five's default secondary position.
dossier.unitFiveW2b: Three velcro entanglement incidents on record. Velcro is no longer issued to Unit Five.
dossier.unitFiveG1b: Named Colonel Flops on day of commissioning. Without authorization. Without hesitation. When asked why, Unit Five looked at Colonel Flops. Accepted as sufficient explanation.
dossier.unitFiveG2b: Every cuddle session with Colonel Flops is 3 minutes and 40 seconds. Without exception. Command has not determined if this is planned. Unit Five has not been asked.
dossier.unitFiveM1b: Visited eastern gate 4 times. Civilian interaction: extremely positive. Three new membership inquiries attributed to Unit Five's presence.
dossier.unitFiveM2b: Fur density contributed to low-profile success. Visited Colonel Flops once during mission. Duration: 3 minutes 40 seconds. Logged.
dossier.unitFiveI1: Velcro entanglement during gear change. Equipment non-functional for 6 minutes. Velcro removed from gear manifest.
dossier.unitFiveI2: Located Colonel Flops 3 minutes 12 seconds after arriving at deployment. New record. Logged. No action taken.
dossier.unitFiveNetworkIntro: Unit Five maintains warm connections across the entire unit. The warmth is genuine. The fur is also warm. These two things are related.
dossier.flopsBalladDesc: The only Wraff Force composition dedicated to a single operative. Begins as a quiet ballad — piano, sparse strings, the musical equivalent of a small brown bear sitting very still at an eastern gate. Builds through commendation, through 47 cuddle sessions with Unit Five, to a full orchestral statement that has been described as surprisingly emotional for a song about a stuffed animal. Colonel Flops has heard this piece. Colonel Flops did not comment. This was expected. It was also correct.
dossier.flopsBalladSongSub: Hybrid Ballad to Orchestral · The Only Song Written For Him
dossier.flopsBalladNote: Sergeant Bork saluted during the first playback. The salute was not requested. Unit Five began the session 14 seconds before the piece ended. Colonel Flops was already at the eastern gate. He did not move. He did not comment. The piece was considered a success.

operations.title: OPERATIONS
operations.lead: Complete archive of all Wraff Force field operations. 21 missions completed. All outcomes documented. Some details redacted for operational security. 73% snack recovery rate maintained across all deployments.
operations.berlinDesc: An unguarded pretzel stand was left unsupervised for 4 minutes and 17 seconds. Three pretzels, one bag of peanuts and one chocolate bar went missing. One unidentified pawprint recovered from the salt shaker. No witnesses. Case closed as unexplained snack loss. Wraff Force founded three weeks later.
operations.folsomDesc: First large-scale public deployment at Folsom Europe. Full snack perimeter established across Fuggerstrasse and Welserstrasse. Colonel Flops deployed to eastern gate. One rabbit approached. The rabbit hesitated. The rabbit withdrew. Unit Two's after-action report remains sealed. Crowd reception: enthusiastic.
operations.folsomOperatives: Treatz · Col. Flops · Unit Two · Sgt. Bork · Cpl. Zoom
operations.easterDesc: Annual chocolate reserve protection deployment. Easter Bunny formally classified as hostile following direct operational contact. Unit Two found in proximity to reserves with gold foil wrapper fragments on paws. Explanation filed: pre-existing environmental contamination. Review ongoing. 73% recovery rate maintained.
operations.easterOperatives: Treatz · Col. Flops · Unit Two · Lt. Sniff · Unit Five
operations.noFoolDesc: Annual April 1st snack integrity operation. Holographic treat protocol reviewed and applied following the June 2025 incident. Treat authenticity confirmed before approach on all occasions. All operatives maintained composure. The June 2025 incident was not repeated. We do not discuss June 2025.
operations.sealedNote: * SUCCESS with noted anomalies. Two files sealed. WF-OP-006 is not discussed.
operations.op005Title: Operation Perimeter Hold — First Field Test
operations.cardBody: 21 completed missions. All outcomes documented. Some details redacted for operational security. 73% snack recovery rate maintained.

incidentReports.title: INCIDENT REPORTS
incidentReports.lead: 23 incidents on record. All explanations technically plausible. All cases reviewed. Some cases ongoing. One file sealed. 17 incidents involve Unit Two. This is noted.
incidentReports.unitTwoBannerText: Unit Two is referenced in 17 of 23 incident reports. All explanations have been filed. None have been disproven. The investigation is ongoing. No conclusion has been reached.
incidentReports.unitTwoIntro: The following 17 incident reports reference Unit Two as primary subject. All explanations were technically plausible at time of filing. Investigation status: ongoing. No conclusion has been reached. Unit Two has been informed. Unit Two filed an explanation. The explanation has been noted.

intelBoard.pageLead: Active investigation file — Unit Two, 02-K9-OPS. 17 incident reports. 0 explanations disproven. 0 conclusions reached. All evidence documented. All connections mapped. Investigation status: ongoing.
intelBoard.bbiLead: Biometric index tracked by Lt. Sniff across 19 recorded measurement occasions. Elevated readings correlate with 89% of snack-adjacent incidents. Unit Two's explanation across all 17 elevated readings: dietary variance. Lt. Sniff's assessment: this is a pattern.
intelBoard.connectionsLead: Connection analysis between Unit Two and all active Wraff Force operatives. All connections documented through incident reports or mission logs. One connection remains unconfirmed. That connection is Colonel Flops.
intelBoard.conclusionSub: 17 reports · 0 disproven · Investigation ongoing · Unit Two: dietary variance
intelBoard.pinSubjectBody: 17 incident reports. 0 disproven. Beef breath index: elevated 89% of occasions.
intelBoard.pinTreatzBody: Filed proximity watch protocol. Maintains professional distance near secured snacks.
intelBoard.pinSniffBody: Primary analyst. 12 scent reports filed. Each concludes: this is a pattern.
intelBoard.pinFolsomBody: After-action report sealed by Unit Two immediately after submission. Contents unknown. Sealing reviewed. Outcome: filed.
intelBoard.pinFoilBody: Gold foil matching chocolate reserve found on Unit Two's paws. Same fragments. Two separate incidents. One explanation: environmental contamination. Filed twice.
intelBoard.pinLookBody: 3 operatives independently documented a specific look near secured snacks. Described as: innocent. Lt. Sniff: that is not what innocent looks like.
intelBoard.pinBBIBody: Elevated on 17 of 19 occasions. Correlation with snack incidents: 89%. Unit Two: dietary variance. Lt. Sniff: this is a pattern.
intelBoard.pinFlopsBody: Silence reviewed 34 times as evidence. No conclusions drawn. The silence remains pending. Colonel Flops has not commented.
intelBoard.pinProxBody: Unit Two arrives at snack-adjacent scenes before other operatives in 100% of documented cases. Before the briefing in at least 2 cases.
intelBoard.pinExpBody: No explanation has been repeated. Each is technically plausible. Lt. Sniff notes: the vocabulary of plausible explanations is extensive.
intelBoard.tl1desc: Wraff Force founded. Unit Two joins 3 weeks later. First BBI reading taken on arrival. Reading: elevated. Unit Two: dietary variance.
intelBoard.tl2desc: Unit Two found within 1 metre of secured biscuit reserve (IR-003). First elevated BBI reading recorded (IR-004). Two separate incidents. Two separate explanations. Investigation not yet initiated.
intelBoard.tl3desc: Unit Two arrives at Operation Crumb Recovery before briefing mentions the location (IR-006). Peanut fragments recovered from paws (IR-007). Second proximity event at same reserve (IR-009). Lt. Sniff initiates tracking log.
intelBoard.tl4desc: BBI reading: critical. Timing: 4 minutes after inventory discrepancy. Same month: WF-TREAT-06 sealed. The June 2025 incident is not discussed. Unit Two was present. Unit Two's file for this period is under review.
intelBoard.tl5desc: Unit Two submits after-action report for Folsom and seals it immediately (IR-012). Gold foil wrapper fragments found on paws post-mission (IR-013). Explanation: environmental contamination. This will happen again in March 2026.
intelBoard.tl6desc: Three operatives independently document the look for the first time (IR-015). Five proximity incidents filed as batch (IR-016) — incidents 10 through 14. Five different explanations. Lt. Sniff: statistically improbable.
intelBoard.tl7desc: Colonel Flops' silence reviewed as potential evidence for the first time (IR-017). Unit Two present during review. Neither commented. The silence of Colonel Flops reviewed 34 times since. No conclusions drawn.
intelBoard.tl8desc: Gold foil fragments recovered again post-Easter (IR-019) — same type, same explanation as IR-013. Unit Two observed near Easter Bunny last known position (IR-020). Three further proximity incidents filed as batch (IR-021). Lt. Sniff's Easter report: 8 pages. New record.
intelBoard.tl9desc: Investigation ongoing. 17 incident reports. 0 explanations disproven. 0 conclusions reached. Unit Two is aware of the investigation. Unit Two filed an explanation. The explanation has been noted.
intelBoard.connTreatzFact1: Filed the Proximity Watch Protocol after incident 3.
intelBoard.connTreatzFact2: Maintains professional distance from Unit Two near secured snacks.
intelBoard.connTreatzFact3: Relationship status: officially strained in snack-adjacent contexts.
intelBoard.connSniffFact1: Filed 12 supplementary scent reports on Unit Two.
intelBoard.connSniffFact2: Each report concludes: this is a pattern.
intelBoard.connSniffFact3: Easter scent report: 8 pages. New record. No conclusion.
intelBoard.connFlopsFact1: Silence reviewed as evidence 34 times. No conclusions.
intelBoard.connFlopsFact2: Unit Two called the silence unhelpful. Noted.
intelBoard.connFlopsFact3: Relationship: unconfirmed. Colonel Flops has not commented.
intelBoard.connBorkFact1: Zero shared incident reports with Unit Two.
intelBoard.connBorkFact2: Eastern perimeter anchor — no snacks assigned to that sector.
intelBoard.connBorkFact3: Analysts note: the only operative with zero overlap.
intelBoard.connZoomFact1: Covered Unit Two's post during 3 proximity incidents.
intelBoard.connZoomFact2: Too fast to be implicated in any snack incidents. Unit Two: noted.
intelBoard.connZoomFact3: No shared reports. Zoom arrives too quickly for overlap.
intelBoard.connMuffinFact1: Both present at Operation Biscuit Perimeter.
intelBoard.connMuffinFact2: Muffin's 3 biscuit incidents occurred in Unit Two's sector.
intelBoard.connMuffinFact3: Analysts note this with caution. Muffin appears genuinely unaware.
intelBoard.hintText: One related file exists outside this board's classification level. Access requires a code. The code is hidden on this site. It is not a coincidence that the Easter Bunny is not on this board. She was considered. She was filed elsewhere. Those who look carefully will find the file. Those who find it: proceed with decorum.
intelBoard.pinClassifiedBody: One hostile entity is not on this board. She was filed separately. Access code required.

fieldManual.title: FIELD MANUAL
fieldManual.lead: The official Wraff Force operational field manual. 12 regulations governing all K9 unit field activity. Three appendices. Completion status: 94%. The squeaky toy section has not been started. This is acknowledged.
fieldManual.appADesc: Defines the W-Word Protocol and its classification as a Category E environmental perimeter departure. All W-word triggered absences are automatically reclassified. Sergeant Bork has not read this appendix. It was written for Sergeant Bork.
fieldManual.appBDesc: Appendix B covers classified operational protocols. Access requires clearance above standard level. Full contents are redacted at all available clearance levels. Requests have been filed. Responses have not been received.
fieldManual.appCDesc: Appendix C covers holographic treat identification, authentication procedures and response protocols. Five bullet points are available. The final bullet point references June 2025. The final bullet point is not available.
fieldManual.appCp1: All treats must be physically confirmed before interaction.
fieldManual.appCp2: Holographic treats will not yield to scent analysis. Lt. Sniff's report confirmed this.
fieldManual.appCp3: Visual shimmer at the edges indicates holographic nature. Approach with caution.
fieldManual.appCp4: If uncertain, do not interact. File a report. Wait for authentication clearance.
fieldManual.appCp5: In the event of the June 2025 scenario —

anthemHall.eyebrow: K9 Special Unit — Official Audio Archive
anthemHall.title: ANTHEM HALL
anthemHall.lead: Official audio archive of Wraff Force. Two ceremonial hymns available for direct playback. Six additional field compositions available via the official SoundCloud archive. Use of these recordings is restricted to: ceremonies, mission briefings, dramatic slow-motion corridor walks, and any situation where the gravity of snack security must be musically underscored.
anthemHall.hymnsLead: Two recordings have been designated as official Wraff Force hymns. Both are cleared for ceremonial use, public deployment and internal briefings. Playback during snack incidents is permitted but not required. It is, however, recommended.
anthemHall.h1desc: The official flag anthem of Wraff Force. This recording is designated for use during formal ceremonies, commendation proceedings, flag-raising operations and any occasion that requires the full weight of the Wraff Force mandate to be felt. The composition opens with a sweeping orchestral statement, builds through a central movement that captures the dignity of the K9 operational mission, and closes with a resolution that has been described as profoundly serious given the subject matter. Colonel Flops has heard this anthem. Colonel Flops did not comment. The anthem was considered successful.
anthemHall.h2desc: The official tactical field theme of Wraff Force. Designated for use during mission briefings, pre-deployment gear checks and synchronized team movements through operationally significant locations. Also approved for dramatic slow-motion corridor walks, staircase descents and any moment where operatives need to feel the full cinematic weight of their snack security mandate. The composition builds from a tense opening through a rising central arc to a full orchestral deployment sequence. It has been played during three separate Wraff Force operations. Recovery rate on all three: 73%. The anthem is not considered responsible for this. The anthem has been cleared.
anthemHall.s1desc: The original Wraff Force field anthem. Synthwave pulse underneath a rising orchestral metal framework. Written to accompany rapid snack perimeter deployment operations. The title references the primary tactical asset of any K9 operative: the tail. Used during early Wraff Force drills when the unit had fewer operatives and considerably more optimism about the 73% recovery rate.
anthemHall.s2desc: A cinematic orchestral piece written to document the scenario no Wraff Force operative ever wants to face: a single, unguarded snack. Surrounded. Unprotected. The clock ticking. The composition captures the full dramatic weight of this scenario. It has been described as unexpectedly moving given the subject matter. Unit Two has requested not to be near speakers when this track is played. The request was noted.
anthemHall.s3desc: The full heroic theme of Wraff Force as an institution. Where the Grand Unit Anthem is ceremonial, the Hero Anthem is operational — it was composed to accompany the act of being a Wraff Force operative in the field. Rising brass, driving percussion and a central melodic statement that communicates, in musical terms, that the snack will be secured. It has been played at the beginning of Operation Folsom. The rabbit hesitated. Coincidence has not been ruled out.
anthemHall.s4desc: The official Wraff Force morning reveille. A full military march composition for the start of any operational day. Snare drum, brass, the unmistakable forward momentum of a unit that has somewhere important to be. Recommended for use at mission briefing start, pre-patrol activation, and any morning when operatives require additional motivation to leave their assigned sleeping positions. Sergeant Bork responded well to early playtests. He was already standing by the door.
anthemHall.s5desc: Composed specifically for the annual chocolate reserve protection deployment. The piece opens with pastoral strings — deceptively peaceful, appropriate for a spring operation — before transitioning into a full defensive orchestral sequence as the Easter Bunny is formally classified as hostile. The climax features a percussive sequence that has been cross-referenced with the foil wrapper incident in WF-IR-019. The cross-reference was not causal. It was noted anyway.
anthemHall.s6desc: The official musical documentation of Operation Folsom — Wraff Force's first large-scale public deployment. The piece captures the full scope of Fuggerstrasse: the crowd, the perimeter, the eastern gate. A sustained held note during the central movement has been identified as the musical moment corresponding to the rabbit's hesitation. This identification was made by Lt. Sniff. Lt. Sniff filed a report about it. The report was 3 pages long.
anthemHall.s7desc: A military thriller orchestral piece for the April 1st snack integrity operation. Tense strings, strategic silence and a central motif built around deception and authentication. The holographic treat protocol is represented musically by a shimmering string passage that sounds almost real but is not quite. This was intentional. The composition concludes with a full resolution — the treat was authenticated, the mission succeeded. The piece does not reference June 2025. This was also intentional.
anthemHall.s8desc: The only composition in the Wraff Force archive dedicated to a single operative. Begins as a quiet ballad — piano, sparse strings, the musical equivalent of a small brown bear sitting very still at an eastern gate. Builds gradually through commendation, through the unit's growth, through 47 cuddle sessions with Unit Five, to a full orchestral statement that has been described as surprisingly emotional for a song about a stuffed animal. Colonel Flops has heard this piece. Colonel Flops did not comment. This was expected. It was also correct.
anthemHall.scDesc: All 8 Wraff Force compositions are available in the official SoundCloud archive. Access is unrestricted. Clearance is still recommended. The archive is maintained by primary operative Puppy Treatz. Unit Two's listening history has been reviewed. It was not helpful.
anthemHall.cardBody: Officially recognized Wraff Force anthems. For use during ceremonies, briefings and dramatic slow-motion corridor walks only.
anthemHall.subtitle: Official Audio Files

status.title: STATUS
status.sub: All Systems Monitored · Live Feed · Last Updated: Now

classified.gateDesc: This file is classified. Access requires a valid clearance code. The code exists. It can be found.
classified.gateHint: The code is hidden somewhere on this site. Those who look carefully will find it. Those who don't: routine patrols only.
classified.heroAlias: Known As: "Der Osterhase" · WF-HOSTILE-01 · Threat Classification: Seasonal / Persistent
classified.audioDesc: The following audio file was composed in direct response to Bärbel's recurring hostile activity. It classifies the Easter Bunny as a hostile entity for the first time in an official Wraff Force recording. This was a significant moment. The rabbit has been informed. The rabbit has not responded.
classified.audioSongSub: Epic Cinematic Orchestral · First Official Hostile Classification of Bärbel
classified.seasonDesc: Bärbel exhibits a highly predictable seasonal cycle. Documented across 4 consecutive years. Wraff Force threat assessment is adjusted quarterly. The pattern has not changed. Bärbel has been informed that we are aware of the pattern. Bärbel did not seem concerned.
classified.conclusionSub: She will return next Easter · The foil will match · The paws will have been there first
classified.audioDescText: The following audio file was composed in direct response to Bärbel's recurring hostile activity. It classifies the Easter Bunny as a hostile entity for the first time in an official Wraff Force recording. This was a significant moment. The rabbit has been informed. The rabbit has not responded.
classified.seasonDescText: Bärbel exhibits a highly predictable seasonal cycle. Documented across 4 consecutive years. Wraff Force threat assessment is adjusted quarterly. The pattern has not changed. Bärbel has been informed that we are aware of the pattern. Bärbel did not seem concerned.
classified.s1desc: Unauffällig. Occasionally spotted near confectionery wholesalers. No confirmed incidents. Wraff Force: monitoring only.
classified.s2desc: Annual gym registration. Same facility, four years running. Motivational hoodie: "NEW YEAR NEW ME." Weight: noticeably elevated. Determination: genuine. Results: temporary.
classified.s3desc: Weight stabilizing. Supply chain contacts reactivated. gold foil procurement begins. Wraff Force Treat Alert Level elevated. Bärbel: increasingly confident. Critically.
classified.s4desc: Full operational deployment. Snack infiltration rate: critical. gold foil dispersal: widespread. Known locations: everywhere. Wraff Force on highest alert. The rabbit knows what it's doing.
classified.s5desc: Disappears within 48 hours of Easter. Next confirmed sighting: always a bar. Always Cologne. Always the same stool. Glitter-Top still on. Oster-Handtasche still present. Mission: complete.
classified.s6desc: Largely inactive. Occasional sightings near snack-adjacent establishments. Reports of extended brunch sessions. Lt. Sniff notes: the smell of confectionery follows her. Always.
classified.b1desc: No one suspects the Easter Bunny. This has been her primary tactical advantage for decades. The Wraff Force is the first agency to formally document it.
classified.b2desc: Supply chain spans at least 4 countries. gold foil sourcing: undocumented. Chocolate procurement contacts: classified. Network structure: unknown. Unit Two: possibly aware.
classified.b3desc: Annual egg acquisition from multiple farms. Most cooperate without incident. The Mertens farm situation was an exception. An injunction was involved. She still gets the eggs.
classified.b4desc: Same gym. Same bar stool. Same foil brand. Same Cologne establishment post-Easter. Wraff Force has mapped her annual movement pattern with 89% accuracy. She is aware of this. She does not care.
classified.b5desc: The motivational hoodie phase produces elevated risk-taking behavior. Three of four known pre-Easter reconnaissance missions were initiated in January. All coincided with gym registration week.
classified.b6desc: Bärbel hesitated once. Once. In front of the eastern gate. In front of Colonel Flops. She did not enter. She has not returned to that sector. Colonel Flops has not commented. He did not need to.
classified.i1desc: Alleged harassment of laying hens during pre-Easter egg acquisition. Bärbel denied presence. Feather traces at scene. Charges dropped — primary witness (rooster) declined to cooperate. Case closed. Bärbel still collects eggs from that farm.
classified.i2desc: Interrogated for unauthorized entry to a chocolate manufacturing facility in Cologne. Released — insufficient evidence. Gold foil found on paws: "environmental contamination." Factory security reviewed. Bärbel was not added to the visitor list. She was added the following year.
classified.i3desc: Apprehended in Easter egg aisle, 03:40 hrs. Stated: "I work here." Bärbel does not work there. Bärbel has never worked there. Bärbel was asked to leave. Bärbel left. One foil wrapper recovered from the scene. Matched standard Bärbel profile.
classified.i4name: Poultry Farm Mertens, Eifel — Restraining Order
classified.i4desc: Restraining order filed by family Mertens regarding conduct during egg collection visits. Details: sealed. The hens recovered. The rooster did not testify. The injunction limits visit duration to 90 minutes and requires a supervisor to be present. Bärbel complies. Marginally.
classified.ptOccupActual: Snack Infiltration, Gold Foil Supply Chain
classified.ptWFVal: Linked to 4 Wraff Force incidents. Direct involvement: unconfirmed. Foil fragments: confirmed.
classified.ev2ref: Confirmed sighting · Easter +2 days · Oster-Handtasche present
classified.ev3ref: Annual gym registration · "NEW YEAR NEW ME" · Threat rising
classified.ev4ref: WF-IR-013 + WF-IR-019 · Same brand · Same operative's paws
classified.ptRealNameNote: (last name: 3 variations on file, none verified)

contact.title: CONTACT
contact.lead: Direct contact with primary operative Puppy Treatz, 09-K9-OPS. All channels are monitored. Response times vary depending on proximity to active snack scenarios. Average response time: reasonable. Treat bribes are not required. They are, however, noted.
contact.instagramDesc: Primary public channel. Operational updates, field imagery, pupplay community.
contact.emailDesc: Official operations channel. Formal inquiries, incident reports, collaboration requests.
contact.telegramDesc: Direct line to primary operative. For field communications, mission coordination and treat-adjacent emergencies.
contact.note: Wraff Force is part of the pupplay community in North Rhine-Westphalia and beyond. If you are a pup, handler, or fellow operative looking to connect — all channels are open. Unit Two contact requests are reviewed with additional scrutiny. This is standard procedure.
contact.operativeNote: Primary operative and founding member of Wraff Force. Active in the pupplay community in NRW and across Europe. Open to contact with fellow pups, handlers and operatives worldwide. Initial contact: may be reserved. Subsequent contact: significantly less reserved. This is documented in the dossier.
contact.recruitBody: Official applications are accepted. An aptitude test is required. All results will be published. The applicant list is publicly accessible.
contact.recruitNote: Note: Unit Two has also applied. Their file is on record. The evaluation was not surprising.

verify.title: CREDENTIAL VERIFICATION NODE
verify.scanNote: QR input received. Initializing secure K-9 link…
verify.localOnly: This node performs a local verification only. No data is transmitted.
verify.matchNote: Identity hash matches WRAFF FORCE K-9 registry. Operative cleared for dossier access.

notFound.title: THIS SECTOR DOES NOT EXIST
notFound.desc: The requested sector could not be located in the Wraff Force registry. Either the sector was never established, has been sealed following a June 2025 incident, or Unit Two was involved. A report has been filed. Return to HQ immediately.
notFound.unitTwo: Unit Two has been notified of this error. Unit Two's explanation: dietary variance.

ir.ut003desc: Unit Two found within 1 metre of secured biscuit reserve. Explanation: checking perimeter integrity from the inside. Accepted. This was incident one of seventeen.
ir.ut004desc: Lt. Sniff recorded first elevated beef breath reading following a snack inventory discrepancy. Unit Two: dietary variance. Index tracking initiated.
ir.ut006title: First to Arrive — Crumb Recovery Scene
ir.ut006desc: Unit Two arrived at mission location before the briefing mentioned it. Explanation: anticipated the deployment. Command noted the briefing had not yet mentioned the location.
ir.ut007desc: Peanut fragments matching secured reserve inventory found on Unit Two's paws. Explanation: pre-existing contamination. Lt. Sniff confirmed the scent match. Report: 4 pages.
ir.ut009desc: Unit Two found within 1 metre of the same reserve as WF-IR-003. Different explanation filed. Proximity protocol review initiated.
ir.ut011desc: Critical reading 4 minutes after inventory discrepancy. Unit Two: dietary variance. Lt. Sniff: this is incident eleven. Review initiated for the third time.
ir.ut012desc: Unit Two submitted after-action report for Operation Folsom. Report sealed immediately. Contents unknown. Sealing authorized by Unit Two. Review outcome: filed.
ir.ut013desc: Gold foil fragments matching secured chocolate reserve found on Unit Two post-Folsom. Explanation: environmental contamination. Lt. Sniff filed a separate report.
ir.ut015desc: Three operatives reported Unit Two gave a specific look near secured snacks. Described as: innocent. Lt. Sniff: that is not what innocent looks like.
ir.ut016desc: Five proximity incidents filed as batch. Five different explanations. All plausible. Lt. Sniff: statistically improbable. Unit Two: coincidence is underappreciated.
ir.ut017desc: Colonel Flops silence reviewed as evidence. Unit Two present. Neither commented. Both silences noted. The silence remains the most reviewed document in the case.
ir.ut019desc: Same foil fragment type as WF-IR-013 recovered post-Easter. Same explanation filed. Lt. Sniff noted the annual recurrence. Report: 8 pages. New record.
ir.ut020desc: Unit Two observed near Easter Bunny's last known position after hostile classification. Command: this was not assigned. Unit Two: it felt like the right thing to do.
ir.ut021desc: Three further proximity incidents filed. Each has a separate explanation. No explanation repeats. Command noted Unit Two appears to have an extensive vocabulary of plausible explanations.
ir.g001desc: Pretzel stand unsupervised 4 min 17 sec. Three pretzels, peanuts, chocolate bar missing. Pawprint in salt. Explanation: I was in the area. Founded Wraff Force.
ir.g002desc: Squeaky toy activated eleven times by Corporal Zoom during night operation. Toy secured and filed as tactical equipment. Field Manual squeaky toy section remains unwritten.
ir.g004desc: Sgt. Bork abandoned post after civilian said going for a walk. Absence: 12 minutes. W-word protocols introduced. The word walk removed from all briefing documents.
ir.g005desc: Mission delayed 22 minutes. Lt. Sniff analyzed a non-mission-relevant cheese biscuit. Report: 4 pages. Described as highly complex. Command accepted this.
ir.g008desc: File sealed. WF-TREAT-06. Not available at standard clearance. We do not discuss June 2025. Operatives seeking information are redirected to the snack inventory checklist.
ir.g010desc: Colonel Flops commissioned without review. Named without authorization. Eastern gate assignment verbal. All paperwork filed retroactively. Status: closed.
ir.g014desc: Three biscuits consumed during active duty. Three reports. Three explanations: it was an accident. All three times. Probationary review initiated.
ir.g018desc: Fur entangled with velcro during gear change. Equipment non-functional 6 minutes. Velcro removed from Unit Five's manifest. The fur remains.
ir.g022desc: Colonel Flops removed from eastern gate 4 minutes for cleaning. Three operatives felt watched during his absence. He was not yet back. Under review.
ir.g023desc: Door incident 14. Glass door, not visible at approach velocity. Annex 7-A revision initiated for the 14th time. Enthusiasm: unaffected. Door replaced. Annex pending.

fm.r1p1: All snacks within a designated Wraff Force perimeter are considered mission-critical assets. They are to be treated with the same level of seriousness as any tactical objective. No snack is to be dismissed as trivial, regardless of size, flavour or caloric content.
fm.r1p2: An unsecured snack is a compromised snack. A compromised snack is a failed mission. A failed mission is documentation. All documentation is filed. All filed documentation is reviewed. The review takes time. Time is operational cost. The snack was not worth it.
fm.r1note: This regulation has never been violated without a subsequent incident report. The two events are considered connected. This has not been formally stated. It is understood.
fm.r2p1: The official Wraff Force snack recovery benchmark is 73%. This figure was established following the first operational assessment and has not been revised. It will not be revised. 73% represents acceptable operational performance.
fm.r2p2: The benchmark of 73% applies across all mission types, all operatives and all snack categories. There are no exceptions. There are no adjustments for seasonal snacks, event-specific treats or chocolate reserves. 73% is 73%.
fm.r2note: The anomaly mission WF-OP-016 recorded 100% recovery. This was filed under: Anomaly. The benchmark remains 73%. The anomaly has not affected the benchmark. The benchmark does not acknowledge the anomaly.
fm.r3p1: All commissioned ranks within Wraff Force are to be acknowledged and respected regardless of the nature of the commission, the circumstances of the commissioning, or whether the commissioning was formally authorized at the time. This regulation applies retroactively.
fm.r3p2: The rank of Colonel, even when conferred upon a stuffed bear without documentation, carries full operational weight. The bear does not need to acknowledge the rank. The rank stands. Sergeant Bork has saluted on three separate occasions without response.
fm.r3note: Colonel Flops' commissioning paperwork was filed retroactively. All paperwork is now fully in order. The rank is valid. The question of whether Colonel Flops is aware of the rank has not been formally addressed.
fm.r4p1: All incidents, regardless of severity, classification, or plausibility of the attached explanation, are to be formally filed. There is no minimum threshold for filing. A suspicion is sufficient. A feeling is sufficient. A smell is sufficient. Lt. Sniff has used all three.
fm.r4p2: Incidents are filed, reviewed and stored. They are never deleted. They are never expired. An incident from March 2025 carries the same weight as an incident from March 2026. Every filed incident is a permanent record.
fm.r4note: 23 incidents are currently on file. 17 involve Unit Two. 0 have been deleted. All remain active. The oldest incident is WF-IR-001, March 2025. It is still open. It founded this agency.
fm.r5p1: A Wraff Force perimeter is a commitment. Once established, it is to be maintained until formally stood down by the primary operative. No operative may abandon their assigned perimeter position without authorisation. Exceptions are documented in Appendix A.
fm.r5p2: Perimeter breaches are classified by cause. Environmental breaches are filed under Category E. Operative-initiated departures are filed under Category O. The term walkies does not appear anywhere in this regulation. This was deliberate.
fm.r5note: Appendix A includes the W-Word Protocol, introduced following WF-IR-004. The protocol reclassifies all walk-related departures as Category E — Environmental. Sergeant Bork has not read Appendix A. The protocol was written for him.
fm.r6p1: Effective July 2025, all treats must be authenticated before consumption or interaction. Authentication includes: visual confirmation, scent analysis by a qualified operative, and cross-reference with the active snack inventory log. Shortcuts are not permitted.
fm.r6p2: The treat authentication protocol was introduced in response to an incident in June 2025. The nature of that incident is not described in this regulation. It is not described anywhere. Regulation 6 was introduced because of it. That is all that will be said.
fm.r6note: Holographic treats are a known threat vector as of June 2025. The holographic treat protocol is detailed in Appendix C. Appendix C is partially redacted. The relevant section is fully redacted. We do not discuss June 2025.
fm.r7p1: Section 7 has been designated for completion since the founding of Wraff Force. The section covers: approved squeaky toy types, permitted activation frequency, acoustic impact on perimeter operations, and the classification of squeaky toys recovered during active deployment.
fm.r7voidSub: This section has been acknowledged as incomplete in 3 consecutive review periods. Commitment to completion: reiterated. Progress: unchanged.
fm.r7note: Corporal Zoom activated a squeaky toy 11 times during Operation Night Sector Seven. The toy was filed as tactical equipment. There is no regulation governing this. There is no regulation because Section 7 has not been written. These two facts are related.
fm.r8p1: Approved cuddle sessions between operatives, honorary operatives and designated cuddle-command assets are logged as official unit activity. Duration is recorded. Frequency is tracked. Sessions are not mandatory. They are contractual where documented.
fm.r8p2: The standard logged duration for Unit Five and Colonel Flops cuddle sessions is 3 minutes and 40 seconds. This duration has been consistent across all 47 logged sessions. Command has reviewed this consistency. Command has filed it.
fm.r8note: Regulation 8 was not in the original field manual draft. It was added following Unit Five's first deployment. The addition was approved without formal debate. Colonel Flops did not object. Colonel Flops has not objected to anything.
fm.r9p1: No operative under active proximity watch protocol may approach within 2 metres of a secured snack reserve without prior authorisation. This regulation applies regardless of explanation, stated intent or history of technically plausible excuses.
fm.r9p2: The proximity watch protocol was introduced following the seventh incident involving a specific operative who is referenced in 17 of 23 incident reports. The regulation does not name that operative. Everyone knows.
fm.r9note: The proximity protocol has been active since May 2025. It has not reduced incident frequency. It has increased documentation quality. Lt. Sniff considers this an improvement. Unit Two has not commented.
fm.r10p1: The eastern gate is a designated permanent post. It is to be staffed at all times during active deployment. The assigned operative for the eastern gate is Colonel Flops, CF-01, who has occupied this post without interruption since May 2025.
fm.r10p2: Colonel Flops' presence at the eastern gate has been shown to have a measurable deterrent effect. The mechanism has not been formally explained. The rabbit hesitated. That is sufficient documentation.
fm.r10note: Colonel Flops was temporarily removed from the eastern gate for 4 minutes during perimeter cleaning. Three operatives independently reported feeling watched. He was not yet back. Regulation 10 now includes a minimum reinstatement clause.
fm.r11p1: All doors on active deployment sites are to be assessed before rapid entry. Assessment includes: visibility check, clearance confirmation, and velocity calibration. No operative is to approach a door at a speed that exceeds the door's safe clearance rate.
fm.r11p2: This regulation was introduced following 14 separate door-related incident reports involving the same operative. The regulation does not name the operative. The regulation does name the doors. Two have been replaced. The operative's enthusiasm has not been affected.
fm.r11note: Annex 7-A provides detailed door assessment protocols. Annex 7-A has been under revision since June 2025. Annex 7-A and Section 7 are the two incomplete items. A connection has been suggested. The connection has not been confirmed.
fm.r12p1: Regulation 12 is the final and binding regulation of the Wraff Force Field Manual. It is brief. It is unambiguous. It requires no appendices. It requires no clarification. It requires no discussion.
fm.r12note: All questions regarding June 2025 are to be redirected to Appendix C. Appendix C is partially available. The relevant section is not available. The relevant section was sealed following WF-TREAT-06. We do not discuss WF-TREAT-06 either.

apply.title: APPLICATION
apply.lead: Official applications for the Wraff Force K9 unit. Completion of the aptitude test is mandatory. All results will be published. Honesty is recommended. It will be verified regardless.

All answers are evaluated through the official Wraff Force assessment protocol. Dishonest statements will be detected. Results are public. Unit Two's application is already on file. It was not helpful.
